Southside Civil Pty Ltd was established in 2018 by Michael Torres to provide civil earthworks, drainage installation, and road construction services to councils and developers across South East Queensland. The company operated from a yard at 15 Industrial Drive, Rocklea QLD 4106.

The company initially traded profitably, winning several council contracts for subdivision earthworks and stormwater drainage installation. In early 2023, the company was engaged as a subcontractor on the Greenfield Estate development, a large residential subdivision requiring bulk earthworks, road base preparation, and stormwater infrastructure.

The principal contractor, Meridian Developments Pty Ltd, entered voluntary administration in September 2023, owing Southside Civil approximately $185,000 for completed works. This debt was never recovered. The loss of this receivable, combined with the cost of plant and equipment committed to the project, placed severe strain on the company cash flow.

Following the Meridian collapse, the company was unable to meet its quarterly BAS obligations. The ATO issued amended assessments for income tax of $312,450 and an ICA deficit of $87,600. Superannuation guarantee charges of $45,200 also remained outstanding.

Trade creditors including Coates Hire ($28,750 for equipment rental), Boral Limited ($67,320 for concrete and aggregate supply), and Hanson Construction Materials ($41,890 for road base materials) were placed on stop credit, limiting the company ability to tender for new works.

The Torres Family Trust, an entity controlled by Mr Torres, had provided operational funding of $85,000 during the Meridian dispute. This debt has been forgiven and is excluded from the restructuring plan.

Mr Torres has secured three new council maintenance contracts commencing January 2026, providing stable recurring revenue of approximately $45,000 per month. The company has reduced its fixed costs by downsizing from 12 to 7 employees and selling surplus plant. Mr Torres has committed to a personal contribution of $380,000 over 24 months to fund the restructuring plan.
